title: Auto-update (and rollback?) failed for a fredy container crash
body:
### Issue Description

I had fredy running with `AutoUpdate=registry` in a Quadlet, but after a crash in a version (see https://github.com/orangecoding/fredy/issues/341) it seems to have stopped updating.

Container: https://github.com/orangecoding/fredy/pkgs/container/fredy

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```ini
~/.config/containers/systemd$ cat fredy.container 
[Container]
AutoUpdate=registry
HealthCmd='curl --fail --silent --show-error --max-time 5 http://localhost:9998/ || exit 1'
HealthInterval=120s
HealthRetries=1
HealthStartPeriod=10s
HealthTimeout=10s
Image=ghcr.io/orangecoding/fredy
PublishPort=9998:9998
Volume=/var/home/c-fredy/container-compose-collection/fredy/conf:/conf:ro,Z
Volume=db:/db

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Since at least May 06 19:10:53.

### Describe the results you received

It has stopped updating at v20.0.0 (see https://github.com/orangecoding/fredy/issues/341) apparently. Maybe because v20.0.0 has introduced a crash.

https://github.com/orangecoding/fredy/pkgs/container/fredy/725764232?tag=20.0.0 "was published on Mar 9." though which is confusing.

Because I see an image pull and refresh on May 06 19:10:54 and even on Jun 12 03:14:08, but for some reason it could not update until a fixed version?

### Describe the results you expected

It should update to the latest version. A manual update procedure like this fixed it then:
```
podman pull ghcr.io/orangecoding/fredy:latest
podman auto-update
```

Alternatively, rollback? But this apparently also did not work.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

```
$ rpm-ostree status -b
State: idle
AutomaticUpdatesDriver: Zincati
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
